page: Microsoft Azure E64-16ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the EDSv4 family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. The instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 504.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 31.5 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, providing 2458 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average tier across multiple workloads. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is recorded at 1486.38, while multi-core performance reaches 15092.71. Memory bandwidth testing shows 842.48 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 101.42 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In database operations, the instance achieves a PassMark database score of 7126.2 and handles 14.55 million Redis SET operations per second. Large-model LLM inference presents a performance tradeoff, with 7B model prompt processing falling into the weak category at 29.58 tokens/sec, though text generation remains average at 11.32 tokens/sec.

The high memory density relative to the vCPU count provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require massive RAM footprints without needing proportional scaling of compute cores. This resource balance makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive applications, relational database management systems, in-memory caching layers, and large-scale data analytics. However, the lack of hardware acceleration limits its utility for heavy machine learning training, and the moderate CPU clock speed of 2.6 GHz may constrain compute-bound tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv4 family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, offering a high memory-to-core ratio of 31.5 GB, alongside 2458 GB of local storage. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most categories, with a PassMark database score of 7126.2 and 14.55 million Redis SET operations per second. However, LLM inference on medium 7B models shows a performance tradeoff, with prompt processing falling into the weak tier at 29.58 tokens/sec. This instance provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high compute scaling, making it suitable for large databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, designed for large databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density, local storage, and balanced performance for database and caching workloads.
